task:
Regenerate the priority-set staleness audit. This is READ-AND-RECOMMEND ONLY: do NOT edit, archive, redefine, move, or reorder any priority; do NOT touch product code. The only file you write is the audit table itself.

GOAL: Produce ONE founder-decision table at `cocoder/priorities/audits/latest-audit.md` (overwrite it) that assesses every active priority and every backlog item against the CURRENT built state, so the founder can act in one read.

SITE SET — assess exactly these (re-confirm by `ls cocoder/priorities/` and `ls cocoder/priorities/backlog/` before you start; do not trust this list if the tree differs):
  Active priority files (cocoder/priorities/*.md): ad-hoc-session-architecture.md, adhoc-session.md, AGENTS.md, new-primary-root.md, oz-terminal.md, priority-audit.md
  Backlog (cocoder/priorities/backlog/*.md): agentic-pattern-drift-detection.md, deployment-plays.md, multi-repo-commit-spine.md, priority-architecture-contract.md, quinn-app-testing.md, research-sandboxing.md, run-tests-required-checkpoint.md, runner-decoupling-progress.md, runner-decoupling-refactor.md

FOR EACH item produce a row with these columns: (1) Priority/item name; (2) Status-vs-reality read — what its file claims vs what the current code, PLAYBOOK, SESSION_LOG, ADRs, and order.json actually show; (3) Recommended disposition — exactly one of keep-active / archive / redefine / merge / promote / demote; (4) One-line reason; (5) Dangling reference or conflict found (or 'none').

GROUND TRUTH to read before judging: cocoder/priorities/order.json, cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md, cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md (recent entries), cocoder/decisions/ (ADR statuses — note any superseded/accepted relevant to a priority), the prior audit cocoder/priorities/audits/latest-audit.md (for continuity; re-verify, do not copy stale rows), and grep the code where a priority claims an outcome that may already be built.

MUST EXPLICITLY FLAG (these are known smells — confirm or refute each in the table, do not skip):
  - order.json lists [priority-audit, new-primary-root, ad-hoc-session-architecture, oz-terminal] but the priorities/ dir also contains adhoc-session.md and AGENTS.md, which are NOT in order.json. Are they orphans, superseded, or intentionally unordered? Recommend a disposition.
  - ad-hoc-session-architecture.md vs adhoc-session.md — same concern? merge/redefine/archive one?
  - runner-decoupling-progress.md (~144KB) vs runner-decoupling-refactor.md in backlog — duplication/merge candidate?
  - priority-audit.md is the STANDING meta-priority — it is never archived; confirm keep-active and that its 'Latest run pickup' is current.

OUTPUT FORMAT: a single markdown table actionable in one read, preceded by a one-paragraph summary stating the headline (how many keep-active vs archive/redefine/merge candidates, and the most important conflict found) and the date of this audit (2026-06-29). Conclusion-first, terse. Keep the file self-contained.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: the file contains one row for every site-set item above (15 rows); every row has all 5 columns filled; every disposition is one of the six allowed values; the four MUST-FLAG smells are each addressed by name; no product code or priority file other than the audit was modified (`git status` shows only cocoder/priorities/audits/latest-audit.md changed). MUST NOT: enact any disposition, edit order.json, or move any file.
